modules.name: AuditDashboard
modules.description: Audit program overview with stats and calendar

modules.name: AuditRegister
modules.description: Full list of all audits with filtering

modules.name: AuditDetail
modules.description: Full audit workspace: planning, fieldwork, findings, reports

modules.name: FindingsTracker
modules.description: Cross-audit findings tracker and remediation management

modules.name: MyActions
modules.description: Remediation actions assigned to current user

events.name: finding:created
events.description: Notify finding owner and auditee of new finding

events.name: finding:remediated
events.description: Notify auditor when management marks finding remediated

events.name: finding:verified
events.description: Notify owner when auditor verifies remediation

events.name: audit:status_changed
events.description: Notify team members when audit advances stage

events.name: remediation:overdue
events.description: Notify assignee when action becomes overdue

flows.name: Conduct Audit
flows.trigger: Audit manager creates and kicks off audit
steps: Create audit with status = planned, assign lead auditor and team
Advance to fieldwork → conduct interviews, gather evidence
Create AuditFindings as issues are identified
Advance to review → internal team review of findings
Advance to draft_report → share findings with management for response
Advance to final_report → generate and upload final report
Advance to closed → all findings must have due dates set

flows.name: Remediate Finding
steps: Owner creates one or more RemediationActions with assignees and due dates
Auditor verifies remediation (reviews evidence, sets verified_at)
If verified → set finding status = verified
If not accepted → send back with comments

computed_fields.name: finding_count
computed_fields.formula: COUNT(audit_findings) WHERE audit_id = audits.id AND deleted_at IS NULL

computed_fields.name: open_finding_count
computed_fields.formula: COUNT(audit_findings) WHERE audit_id = audits.id AND status IN ('open','in_remediation','overdue')

computed_fields.name: overdue_findings
computed_fields.formula: COUNT findings WHERE due_date < TODAY AND status NOT IN ('verified','accepted')

domain_rules: Audit number auto-increments per org: AUD-0001, AUD-0002
Finding number auto-increments per org: FND-0001, FND-0002
Audit status follows a linear pipeline; cannot skip stages without admin override
Findings cannot be deleted after audit reaches final_report status
Repeat findings require a link to the prior finding for tracking
Management response must be submitted before audit can advance to final_report
Finding verification can only be done by audit team members, not by the auditee
Closed audits are read-only; require admin to reopen
Documents are versioned; uploading a new version of same type increments version number
generation_rules: Audit status machine enforces linear progression: planned→in_progress→fieldwork→review→draft_report→final_report→closed
Report generation uses PDF template with org logo, findings table, severity heatmap, and executive summary
Finding 4C structure (condition, criteria, cause, effect) renders as labeled sections in detail view
Overdue detection cron job runs daily: marks findings where due_date < TODAY and status not verified/accepted
Auditee portal: limited-permission view to see their findings and submit management responses
Document versioning: when same document_type is uploaded again, increment version and keep history
AuditCalendarView uses Gantt-style bars per audit with fieldwork phase highlighted differently
Soft delete only on all entities; audit archive preserves full historical record

org_id_injection.middleware: req.body.org_id is stripped; injected from req.user.org_id

conflict_response.message: Record was modified by another user. Refresh and retry.

concurrency.high_contention_tables: see entities with high write concurrency

audit_trail.triggers: application-level (before write hook) — never DB trigger to avoid log bloat

standard_constraints.name: chk_{table}_deleted_order
standard_constraints.expression: deleted_at IS NULL OR deleted_at >= created_at

db_constraints_policy.enum_validation: DB ENUM types preferred over VARCHAR + CHECK; enum values defined in global enums registry

soft_delete_policy.filter: deleted_at IS NULL applied globally via query builder middleware
soft_delete_policy.cascade: Parent delete soft-deletes children via application cascade, not DB cascade
soft_delete_policy.hard_delete: Prohibited in production; available only via admin CLI with audit log
soft_delete_policy.restore: Set deleted_at = NULL; triggers audit log entry

naming_conventions.date_fields: Always use _at suffix for TIMESTAMPTZ, _date suffix for DATE
naming_conventions.id_fields: Primary key is always id; foreign keys are {entity}_id
